title: anthemis pedunculata subsp. pedunculata
intro: Anthemis pedunculata subsp. pedunculata, commonly known as chamomile, is an annual plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and western Asia. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of chamomile include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation and sleep, and soothing digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome. Chamomile is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and salves, and is also used topically to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne.
